title: A source node forking into a governed arc (green) and a structurally absent path (red dashed) shows that some routes cannot exist — echoing Precept's promise that invalid states are impossible, not merely caught. Thin lines and color-dependent meaning limit its impact as a compact mark.
conceptualBridge: The arc fork means some paths cannot exist, but the composition still explains the product as a flowchart instead of a symbol.
decision: Removed from active set: avg 2.4 is well below the 4.0 threshold, and diagrammatic language fails the conceptual-bridge criterion.
